content.text:
<user_query>
Review proposed implementation steps for the deploy multi-GPU plan. Verify target files and edits are sufficient and no high-risk gaps remain. Focus on correctness of `humanoid-gym/scripts/fuyao_deploy.sh` behavioral changes, CLI semantics, and optional `humanoid-gym/scripts/fuyao_train.sh` distributed side-effect guard. Return blockers only.
</user_query>

1. CRITICAL: `humanoid-gym/scripts/fuyao_train.sh:117` uses a per-process local rendezvous (`--rdzv_endpoint="localhost:${RDZV_PORT}"`) and `RDZV_PORT` is randomly chosen in `humanoid-gym/scripts/fuyao_train.sh:119-123`; for `--nnodes > 1` this is not multi-node safe because each launcher/node cannot rendezvous on a shared endpoint, so distributed startup can hang or split.
2. CRITICAL: `humanoid-gym/scripts/fuyao_train.sh:120-124` launches `torch.distributed.run` with `--nnodes` but no explicit node-rank/cluster coordination inputs in CLI path (no `--node_rank`/reliant on external env) while the deploy side allows multi-node resources; this is a high-risk gap unless the outer runtime is guaranteed to supply consistent rank env consistently across all launchers.
3. BLOCKER: `humanoid-gym/scripts/fuyao_deploy.sh:13` sets `override_seed=true` by default and `humanoid-gym/scripts/fuyao_deploy.sh:298-301` enforces `#seeds == num_jobs`, which makes `--seeds` effectively mandatory; this is a CLI semantics mismatch (help suggests optional seed flag at `-s, --seeds`) and blocks default invocations.
4. BLOCKER: `humanoid-gym/scripts/fuyao_train.sh:53-59`, `103`, and `142-171` contain non-rank/node-gated side effects (artifact backup/symlink, Xrobot POST, evaluate/teleop, finish POST) that are safe only for single-launch execution; in a true multi-node launch model they can run on every node, causing duplicate/corrupt operations (including `ln -s ${job_artifacts_dir}/model ${job_artifacts_dir}/xflow_logs`) and non-idempotent scheduling state.
